description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight, tapering blade that comes to a sharp point. The blade appears metallic and uniform in color, with no visible serrations or decorative elements. The crossguard is simple and straight, extending horizontally to protect the wielder's hand. The handle is cylindrical and ends in a rounded pommel, which is also metallic and unadorned. The overall design is utilitarian, with clean lines and a focus on function rather than ornamentation. The sword's proportions suggest it is designed for one- or two-handed use, typical of medieval European longswords.
description: A straight, double-edged metallic sword with a simple crossguard and rounded pommel, featuring a clean, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, metallic, rounded pommel, no visible grip texture
